code_snippets.kcal_temperature_correlation_check:
kcal_temp_corr = df_train[["kcal_filled", "temperature_capped", "y"]].corr()
print("Correlation Matrix:\n", kcal_temp_corr)

# Create a proxy for food 'heaviness' as suggested by the analyst
df_train["kcal_temp_ratio"] = df_train["kcal_filled"] / (df_train["temperature_capped"] + 1.0)
print("Correlation of kcal_temp_ratio with sales (y):", df_train[["kcal_temp_ratio", "y"]].corr().iloc[0, 1])
code_snippets.negative_residual_remarks_extraction:
significant_neg_residuals = df_train[df_train["ts_residuals"] < df_train["ts_residuals"].quantile(0.1)]
neg_remarks_counts = significant_neg_residuals["remarks_normalized"].value_counts()
print("Remarks patterns on days with high negative residuals:\n", neg_remarks_counts.head(15))

plot_snippets.weather_fried_joint_impact_heatmap:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

pivot_impact = df_train.pivot_table(index="weather_cleaned", columns="is_fried", values="y", aggfunc="mean")
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
sns.heatmap(pivot_impact, annot=True, fmt=".1f", cmap="YlGnBu")
plt.title("Joint Impact of Weather and Fried Food on Average Sales")
plt.show()
plot_snippets.kcal_temperature_sales_scatter:
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
sns.scatterplot(data=df_train, x="temperature_capped", y="kcal_filled", hue="y", size="y", sizes=(20, 200), palette="coolwarm")
plt.title("Relationship between Temperature, Calories, and Sales")
plt.xlabel("Temperature (Capped)")
plt.ylabel("Calories (Kcal)")
plt.legend(title="Sales (y)", bbox_to_anchor=(1.05, 1), loc='upper left')
plt.show()
plot_snippets.residuals_day_of_week_boxplot:
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
sns.boxplot(data=df_train, x="day_of_week", y="ts_residuals", palette="Set3")
plt.axhline(0, color="red", linestyle="--", alpha=0.5)
plt.title("Distribution of Sales Residuals by Day of Week")
plt.show()
plot_snippets.negative_residual_remarks_barplot:
import numpy as np

# Filtering for lowest 15% residuals to see attributes
threshold = df_train["ts_residuals"].quantile(0.15)
underperforming_remarks = df_train[df_train["ts_residuals"] <= threshold]["remarks_normalized"].value_counts().head(10)

if not underperforming_remarks.empty:
    plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
    sns.barplot(x=underperforming_remarks.values, y=underperforming_remarks.index, palette="Reds_r")
    plt.title("Top Remarks Associated with Significant Negative Residuals")
    plt.xlabel("Frequency")
    plt.ylabel("Normalized Remark")
    plt.show()
else:
    print("No specific remarks found for significant negative residuals.")
